When I navigate to a *Contract* record
Then I am able to view all fields
And I am able to edit the following:

* Practice Operations record type: all fields
* Client Contract record type: {color:#bf2600}no fields{color}
* {color:#bf2600}other client contract record types??{color}
* Clinician Contract record type: no fields

When I navigate to a *Clinician* record
Then I am able to view all fields
And I am NOT able to edit any fields

Given I am a Payroll team member
When I navigate to an *Account* record
Then I am able to view all fields
And I am NOT able to edit any fields

1|i0kb3z: 09/Feb/26 10:37 AM *Tester:* Maci Broussard

*Role:* Field Ops/SFO

*Test Case ID:* N/A

*Description:* 

Initiating a Practice Ops change, and system adding rate to existing shift, instead of changing rate on existing shift. For my test to go well, I removed older rate.

*Steps to Reproduce:*

1. Start a contract change on a Service Account
2. Select No for Coverage Change only
3. Summary tab -> Specify Rates
4. Shift, NPPA, Other
5. Select shift and enter new rate
6. Go back to Summary, system displaying shift with both rates instead of updating rate with changes


*Expected vs. Actual:*

Would expect system to update rate instead of adding another rate to one shift. Please review Day 3 and Night 3 for IC NPPAs and Day 1 for MDDOs. The rate change for MDDOs worked as expected (changed rate) but system identified rate change for the IC NPPAs as New instead of Changed.

*Record Link:*

[https://corehc--uat.sandbox.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Contract/800bf00000OcjntAAB/view?ws=%2Flightning%2Fr%2FAccount%2F001bf00000UXvOfAAL%2Fview|https://corehc--uat.sandbox.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Contract/800bf00000OcjntAAB/view?ws=%2Flightning%2Fr%2FAccount%2F001bf00000UXvOfAAL%2Fview]

*Test Case ID:* Gold Set - Row 37

*Description:* 

Receiving an error when trying to specify rates for a PO. Unable to move forward. Was working previously in UAT2 and UAT1.

Connected to Story - PR1050558-2906

Logged in as Lindsey Calbert

Selected Bayou Bend Health System - EM
Clicked "Request Contract Change"
Added a Day 2 shift (169.25 per hour)
Specified same rates for Day 1 for Medical Director and Physician (169.25 per hour)

*Persona/Role:* Recruiter

*Steps to Reproduce:*

1. Log in as Recruiter
2. Find Clinician with Executed contract and with pay codes

1|i0jw4f: 09/Feb/26 10:50 AM *Tester:* Maci Broussard

*Steps to Reproduce:* UAT1: Pay Codes on PO are not getting pulled forward to new (Cloned) PO

1. Log in as SFO
2. Request Contract Change from Service Account (my ex - Woodland Heights MC HM)
3. Once new PO created in Draft, validate no Pay Codes in Rate Pay Codes tab

*Expected VS Actual Result:* Would expect all prior/applicable Pay Codes to pull to new/cloned record. This was functioning in UAT2.

UAT2: Did test for Garnet MC EM. Linked Draft PO here. Details tab has Previous Contract linked. PO in Draft has all Pay Codes from prior PO. [https://corehc--uat2.sandbox.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Contract/800bf00000OOo4JAAT/view?ws=%2Flightning%2Fr%2FAccount%2F001bf00000S3o0KAAR%2Fview|https://corehc--uat2.sandbox.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Contract/800bf00000OOo4JAAT/view?ws=%2Flightning%2Fr%2FAccount%2F001bf00000S3o0KAAR%2Fview]

*Description:* UAT1: record link provided. Draft has no Pay Codes. Prior Executed PO has Pay Codes. NOTE: Adding a few Pay Codes manually to this linked record, so I can continue with my test." In UAT1, when initiating Request Contract Change from Service Account that already had an executed PO, system did not pull forward Pay Codes. User would be required to reconfigure ALL paycodes

Record Link: [https://corehc--uat.sandbox.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Contract/800bf00000OOsMPAA1/view?ws=%2Flightning%2Fr%2FAccount%2F001bf00000UY7dUAAT%2Fview|https://corehc--uat.sandbox.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Contract/800bf00000OOsMPAA1/view?ws=%2Flightning%2Fr%2FAccount%2F001bf00000UY7dUAAT%2Fview]

1|i0jv0v: 03/Mar/26 6:54 AM *Tester:* Kristi Bouillion

*Role:* Data Quality

*Test Case ID:* UAT1- Row 23- New Leased EE - Not Scheduled

*Description:* 

This was working in UAT2-
Today, we were processing a new leased provider in UAT1, and created a new HPF. We were able to process the contract request and got it to the executed state. We checked QGenda Credentialing and confirmed that the provider and his demographic data moved into the system; however, his hospital location was not created in QGenda Credentialing. We refreshed several times and did not see the location in the system.

*Steps to Reproduce:*

Process a new leased provider - create new HPF, new contract


*Expected vs. Actual:*

The location was not pushed into QGenda Credentialing. We would expect both the provider and the location data to be sent to QGenda Credentialing.

*Record Link:* 

E2E Bug: C&CUAT434 UAT1- Leased Provider Location (hospital) Failed to push to QGenda Credentialing

*Description:* 

Job Posting_ UAT 1_Unable to post jobs to job boards_ There is issue when Asymbl UAT 1 URL is loaded

*Steps to Reproduce:*1.Create Job in UAT 1 SF HC Sandbox
2.Make sure to mark check box post externally and select All job boards

1|i0ju1z: 05/Feb/26 3:06 PM *Tester:* Sheila Robson

*Persona/Role:* Payroll

*Test Case ID:* IC Termination

*Description:* Go to CTI record to view the expiration date of an executed CTI.

Expect a future Expiration Date to be populated for the latest executed CTI records. What we are seeing is a blank expiration date on the latest executed CTI records and therefore the termination workflow to Workday will not fire.

CTI Expiration date is one of the requirements to terminate an IC Provider. Not all the latest CTI executed records have expiration dates. Krisiti Bouillon is okay to load 12/31/2026 as the future expiration date for those executed records that do not have an expiration date. The termination integration will not fire without seeing a future Expiration date.

*Record Link:* [https://corehc--uat.sandbox.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Corporation_and_Tax_Information__c/a8sbf000000JUOAAA4/view?ws=%2Flightning%2Fr%2FAccount%2F001bf00000UY7kPAAT%2Fview|https://corehc--uat.sandbox.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Corporation_and_Tax_Information__c/a8sbf000000JUOAAA4/view?ws=%2Flightning%2Fr%2FAccount%2F001bf00000UY7kPAAT%2Fview]

UAT Bug: C&CUAT425 - UAT1 - Data Migration - CTI Expiration Date

 0 Jasmine Moradi 2 Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Bug SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project 03/Feb/26 2:29 PM   5 image-20260129-224546.png 29/Jan/26 3:16 PM SCP Sprint 12.2 (Deploy) 29/Jan/26 6:01 PM The root cause of flow errors was identified as a recursive issue where the flow attempted to update all contracts, including those already canceled, which was fixed by limiting updates to only executed contracts.

1|i0jt2v: 09/Feb/26 7:14 AM *Tester:* Sheila Robson

*Persona/Role:* Payroll

*Description:* Submit Term, Term processed - tasks closed, provider credentials inactivated

Expected the supplier change request to be send to Workday but it was not sent. Terminated Brian Bailey (36157) for 1/28/26. The supplier change request was not in Workday today (1/29/26). According to Amit - it was never sent from HC to Mulesoft to try to process.

1|i0jqgf: 03/Feb/26 7:37 AM When the following criteria is met then the system shall generate an email: (originals stories: [https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-2813|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-2813|smart-link] & [+PR1050558-4405+|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-4405])

* HPF > Anticipated First Shift [First_Scheduled_Date__c] is populated
* HPF Service Account > Billing Company = “EMBCC”
* HPF > Credentialing Status ≠ Inactive

Within the email, the following *Clinician field mapping has to be corrected:*

* Global Provider ID: [Clinician’s Global Provider ID]



Test Data in UAT2 (additional testing info in attached document)

Test Data:
Clinician = [+Karen Erin Lewis+|https://corehc--uat2.sandbox.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Contact/003bf00000XtuGIAAZ/view]

HPF = [+Karen Lewis - Garnet Health Medical Center - EM+|https://corehc--uat2.sandbox.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/0bSbf000000B0ntEAC/view]

*Actual*:      Global Provider ID = 123

*Expected*: Global Provider ID = 934575

[^UAT2 PR1050558-2813 & PR1050558-4405 (1).docx]

 Jasmine Moradi Modify the criteria in the query to pick HPF as per AC 0 Jasmine Moradi 2 Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Story SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project *update to* [https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-4997|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-4997|smart-link]

+AC1: Clinician Contracts can only be requested within HPF Dates+

Given I am a Recruiter in the Corporate Support apps
When I request a contract for a clinician (all employment types and facility titles)
Then I am only able to select HPFs where the HPF Effective From is on or *before* the Start Date of the Contract

_Example 1:_

Hiring EE clinician
HPF 1 Effective From {{2026-02-15}} (from Asymbl process)
Contract needs to start {{2026-02-01}}
HPF 1 won’t be available to select, *Recruiter needs to ask Data Quality to* adjust HPF date to {{2026-02-01}} or earlier then they can adjust/request the contract

_Example 2:_

Updating rates for existing clinician
HPF 1 Effective From {{2020-06-01}} (from data migration)
Contract needs to start {{2026-02-01}}
HPF 1 will be available to select, clinician has been working and just needs new rates 		26/Jan/26 4:36 PM   4 image-20260126-213638.png 22/Jan/26 2:52 PM SCP Sprint 12.1 (UAT), SCP Sprint 12.2 (Deploy) 22/Jan/26 3:49 PM 1|i0jhyf: 12/Feb/26 12:36 PM As a Clinician Contract Specialist, I need HPF and Contract Dates in alignment, so that clinicians are contracted and paid appropriately

UAT Enhancement: HPF Effective Dates UPDATE

None

1|i0jhmf: 27/Feb/26 3:32 AM *Tester:* Ashlee Savoy

*Role:* Recruiter

*Test Case ID:* Gold Set - IC to EE Conversion

*Description:* 

When creating contract request, NPPA tier selection error populated. However, the clinician Raji Suri is a Physician, not a NP/PA. Therefore, this error should not have occurred. Additionally, the drop down did not populate choices in the selection dropdown. See images attached

*Steps to Reproduce:*

Created 3 EE HPFs to replace his 3 IC HPFs

Created a new Contract request for Raja Suri

Select Employee, Full Time

Selected his 3 HPFs for EE Physician

Error received to choose NPPA tier


*Expected vs. Actual:*

Expected: no NPPA tier selection should be present for a Physician, tier selection populates with options
Action: NPPA Tier selection present on Physician, tier selection not populated with options

*Record Link:*

[https://corehc--uat2.sandbox.lightning.force.com/lightning/page/omnistudio/omniscript?omniscript__type=Clinician&omniscript__subType=ContractRequest&omniscript__language=English&omniscript__theme=lightning&omniscript__tabIcon=custom%3Acustom18&omniscript__tabLabel=ContractRequest&c__ContextId=001bf00000S4IXQ&ws=%2Flightning%2Fr%2FAccount%2F001bf00000S4IXQAA3%2Fview|https://corehc--uat2.sandbox.lightning.force.com/lightning/page/omnistudio/omniscript?omniscript__type=Clinician&omniscript__subType=ContractRequest&omniscript__language=English&omniscript__theme=lightning&omniscript__tabIcon=custom%3Acustom18&omniscript__tabLabel=ContractRequest&c__ContextId=001bf00000S4IXQ&ws=%2Flightning%2Fr%2FAccount%2F001bf00000S4IXQAA3%2Fview]

1|i0jhlj: 09/Feb/26 6:42 AM *Tester:* Tara Stogner

*Role:* Clinician Contracting Specialist

*Test Case ID:* Contract 01213395

*Description:* 

I created Conga Agreement, generated the contract, sent the contract via docusign and signed/countersigned the contract. The contract remains in approved status, and no dates populated on the contract record.

*Steps to Reproduce:*

1. Naviage to Elona Bets Contract
2. Create Conga Agreement
3. Generate Contract
4. Send via Docusign.
5. Sign and Countersign Contract


*Expected vs. Actual:*

I would expect the status to move from Approved to Contracting In Progress once the docusign envelope was sent. I would also expect the dates to populate when sent, signed, and countersigned. The status should then update to Executed. None of these things happened.

*Record Link:*

[https://corehc--uat2.sandbox.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Contract/800bf00000NeJI3AAN/view?ws=%2Flightning%2Fr%2FAccount%2F001bf00000UHq94AAD%2Fview|https://corehc--uat2.sandbox.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Contract/800bf00000NeJI3AAN/view?ws=%2Flightning%2Fr%2FAccount%2F001bf00000UHq94AAD%2Fview]

*Description:* 

When navigating back to the assessments list, this warning message populates. The assessment was completed so this message should not populate as there are no changes to be lost. See screenshots

Complete assessment; click assessments list to return to assessment list, warning message present

Expected: this error should populate if the current assessment being worked on is not completed, this should not populate if the assessment has been completed/submitted
Actual: error populating regardless of if forms are submitted/not submitted

Update as per AC for status reference 		0 Robert Christensen 3 Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Story SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project Given I am a Recruiter or Clinician (with portal access)
When I view the At A Glance component
Then I am able to see:

* Reference Check (HC)/ Reference Checks (portal)
** In Progress = bpats__ATS_Applicant__c.References_Complete2__c "*Initiated*"
** Completed = bpats__ATS_Applicant__c.References_Complete2__c "*Completed*"
* -Virtual Interview- (logic already in place)
** -In Progress = bpats__Interview__c.bpats__Location_Type__c = "Online" AND bpats__Interview__c.bpats__Status__c = "Scheduled"-
** -Completed = bpats__Interview__c.bpats__Location_Type__c = "Online" AND bpats__Interview__c.bpats__Status__c = "Feedback Provided"-
* -Onsite Interview- (logic already in place)
** -In Progress = bpats__Interview__c.bpats__Location_Type__c = "In-Person" AND bpats__Interview__c.bpats__Status__c = "Scheduled"-
** -Completed = bpats__Interview__c.bpats__Location_Type__c = "In-Person" AND bpats__Interview__c.bpats__Status__c = "Feedback Provided"- 		19/Feb/26 4:47 PM   10 2dffd591-f37b-4b3c-b251-05d4eb54e90f.PNG 14/Jan/26 6:58 PM SCP HC Sprint 12.4 (R2), SCP Sprint 12.1 (UAT), SCP Sprint 12.2 (Deploy) 15/Jan/26 11:49 AM 1|i0j6mn: 19/Feb/26 4:47 PM *Tester:* Ashlee Savoy

*Description:* The stages on the candidate/clinician progress tracker is not updating properly. Per the screenshots, the reference checks and interview stage statuses on the tracker are not accurate.
References have a status of completed but show in the tracker as in progress
Interview stage has a status of not started but interview feedback was received
*Steps to Reproduce:* Not Provided
*Expected vs. Actual:*
Expected: Status updates on tracker are accurately reflective of current state of the processes
actual: tracker statuses do not match the current state of processes

 Robert Christensen *Platform Event Change:*

Add Case Id to Clinician_Offboarding_Request__e [https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-4705|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-4705|smart-link]

# Type = “Employee”
# Employee ID = Case.Contact.Employee_Id__c
# Termination Date = Case.Termination_Request_Date__c
# Last Day of Work = Case.Last_Day__c
# Primary Reason = Case.Reason_for_Termination__c
# Reason Description = Case.Description
# Pay Through Date = Case.Last_Day__c
# Rehireable__c = Case.Contact.Rehireable__c
# Global Provider Id = Case.Contact.Global_Provider_ID__c
# {color:#ff991f}Case_Id__c = Case.Id{color}

Update 2 flows the send the Platform Event

Case Closure Workday Intimation Flow

Case Creation After Flow



*Case Change:*

New Field: Case.Latest_Integration_Status__c (PickList) (Success/Failed)

*Integration Log:*

Case Id (lookup)

Healthcare Practitioner Facility Id (lookup)

Contract Id (lookup)



Flow in Integration Log on record creation update the corresponding Id field based on type of IF in Related SF Record ID

Expose: Related List in Contract, Case, HPF (Order by Created Date)



*Access:*

Integration Log: Read Only access to Permission set that has access to relative Object Case, HPF, Contract and Edit access for Status
Data Integration: Case like Contract



*Case Closure Workday Intimation Flow*

_For the Default path after Tax Type Decission_

Update the Case : New Field: Case.Latest_Integration_Status__c = Failed

Task for Case Owner:

RT: Default

Subject: Integration Failed

Comments: Issue with CTI Record

Due Date: Today

Status*: Open*



Add a criteria to check for Case.Latest_Integration_Status__c = Failed as OR condition along with

Add a Public Group for Mule to give Case Access

!image-20260121-162232.png|width=905,alt="image-20260121-162232.png"! 		0 Robert Christensen 5 Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Story SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project Updated the solution 26/Jan/26 5:16 PM   11 image-20260121-162232.png 14/Jan/26 6:00 PM SCP Sprint 12.1 (UAT), SCP Sprint 12.2 (Deploy) 15/Jan/26 12:31 AM 1|i0j6lj: 29/Jan/26 9:01 PM *Tester:* Nick Ruiz
*Role:* Human Resources
*Test Case ID:* Gold Set - Row 16 - Termination EE
*Description:* When we put in a salesforce offboarding to term the provider, mulesoft errored out but there is no way to know this as the case was closed like it was successful. Should also be able to retry by editing the offboarding request
*Steps to Reproduce:* Offboard a provider in salesforce
*Expected vs. Actual:* Should show some sort of issue in salesforce on the case that it wasnt successful so the user knows to retry (retry method still under debate)
*Record Link:* Not Provided

As a System Administrator, I need to know if offboarding integrations failed, so that the issue can be resolved and integrations can be attempted again

No permission
 Samantha Fried 0 Samantha Fried 2 Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Bug SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project 04/Feb/26 3:04 PM   7 image-20260112-172627.png 12/Jan/26 12:26 PM SCP Sprint 12.1 (UAT), SCP Sprint 12.2 (Deploy) 12/Jan/26 11:40 PM The issue stemmed from contacts with blank email addresses causing flow errors during reminder processes.
Design: Design Flaws, Data: Data Corruption
1|i0j2hn: 12/Feb/26 3:55 AM When a clinician’s email address is blank on the Contact, and it is time for them to get experience cloud and/or intake form reminders, there is an error message from a flow error. We need to either add validation that requires an email before they get to the experience cloud site in the flow, or that checks for the user’s email address from experience cloud if Contact email is somehow blank (even though I don’t know how the Contact email could be blank if User is created since it creates automatically from the Contact). In short, it’s a data issue but there is improper error handling and/or validation.



!image-20260112-172627.png|width=667,alt="image-20260112-172627.png"!

 Kristin Koide PR1050558-5110, PR1050558-5111 AC1: Use a scheduled flow and move the complex piece to APEX

AC2: To update Status when new HPF Created is Runtime Flow (Key use he HPF.AccountId) Update the field Process_Previous_HPF_Inactivation__c once the contract is processed in the (final method) - Process_Previous_HPF_Inactivation__c will not be updated if the process failed for that contract. 		0 Kristin Koide 8 Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Story SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project +AC1: Update Old HPF When New Clinician Contract Executed+

Given I am a Recruiter in the Corporate Support apps
When a clinician contract has a HPF included where the clinician has an active HPF (Status ≠ ‘Inactive’) for that same Service Account (ex. Facility Title change)
Then when the Contract is Executed the following updates happen automatically to the Old HPF*:

* Contracting Status = ‘Termination In Progress’
* Anticipated Termination Date = <Newly Executed Clinician Contract’s ‘Contract Start Date’ - 1>

And then on the newly executed Clinician Contract ‘Contract Start Date’ (Contract Start Date = Today or in the past), then the system shall update the following fields on the Old HPF*:

* Status = ‘Inactive’
* Contracting Status = ‘Terminated’
* Termination Date = <new contract start date - 1>

*Old HPF record(s) are defined as: the HPF in Status ≠ Inactive for the same Service Account

*
** New HPF is defined as: Provider Network Contract (PNC) record related to the Clinician Contract where ‘Deleted?’ = false/unchecked and has the same service Account as the Old HPF.

Link to notes captured from business on 1/9/2026:
[https://docs.google.com/document/d/138uC9Ifi3MuvWDhge6fgVD3p7C8Wyy4qRj7sG_4PszU/edit?usp=sharing|https://docs.google.com/document/d/138uC9Ifi3MuvWDhge6fgVD3p7C8Wyy4qRj7sG_4PszU/edit?usp=sharing|smart-link]

related stories:

* Date Validation for Manual Create HPF [https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-4980|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-4980|smart-link]
* Date Validation for Automated Create HPF from Job [https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-5090|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-5090|smart-link]
* Adjust Manual Create HPF Experience and HPF View/Edit [https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-5091|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-5091|smart-link] 		26/Jan/26 2:48 PM   12 image-20260120-224320.png 10/Jan/26 6:30 PM SCP Sprint 12.1 (UAT), SCP Sprint 12.2 (Deploy) 13/Jan/26 10:58 AM 1|i0j0oj: 09/Apr/26 2:18 PM As a user within the Corporate Support app, when a Clinician requires a Facility Title change (New HPF with the same service account as a Active HPF) and a new clinician contract is executed, then I want the system to automatically update the old HPF (Status = Active) record to be inactivated by setting key fields, so that when there is a facility title change there are no Effective date gaps between HPF records and statuses are update to reflect the current state.

UAT Enhancement: Clinician Contract - Facility Title Change - HPF Updates to OLD HPF

 harshgupta Contract_Trigger__chn should fire for all

This should only apply for  Contract_HireDate_Trigger__chn 		0 harshgupta 2 Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Story SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project AC1: Workday - Clone flag “Contract_HireDate_Trigger_Ack__c” when Employment Type is the same
Given I am System Administrator
When a *Clinician Contract is cloned* (via ‘Contract Request’ > ‘Start from Previous Active Contract’ OR via Agentforce) with the +*same*+ *Employment Type*
Then they system shall *clone the “Contract_HireDate_Trigger_Ack__c” field* when creating the new Clinician Contract in Status = Draft

AC2: Workday - Do NOT Clone flag “Contract_HireDate_Trigger_Ack__c” when Employment Type is the different
Given I am System Administrator
When a *Clinician Contract is cloned* (via ‘Contract Request’ > ‘Start from Previous Active Contract’ OR via Agentforce) with a +*different*+ *Employment Type*
Then they system shall *NOT* *clone the “Contract_HireDate_Trigger_Ack__c” field* when creating the new Clinician Contract in Status = Draft


AC3: Qgenda - Do NOT Clone flag “Contract_Trigger_Ack__c”
Given I am System Administrator
When a *Clinician Contract is cloned* (via ‘Contract Request’ > ‘Start from Previous Active Contract’ OR via Agentforce) +regardless+ of Employment Type
Then they system shall *NOT* *clone the “Contract_Trigger_Ack__c” field* when creating the new Clinician Contract in Status = Draft 		26/Jan/26 3:05 PM   3 image-20260110-113259.png 10/Jan/26 5:04 AM SCP Sprint 12.1 (UAT), SCP Sprint 12.2 (Deploy) 10/Jan/26 7:57 AM 1|i0j0mz: 29/Jan/26 9:01 PM As a system administrator, when a Clinician Contract is cloned with the same Employment Type (either via ‘Contract Request’ guided experience OR Agentforce), then the system shall clone “Contract_HireDate_Trigger_Ack__c” field value, so that when the new Clinician Contract is Executed we will use this indicator to determine to send trigger for Workday ([+PR1050558-4861+|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-4861])

NOTE: the Workday flag should not be cloned if the Employment Type is different/changed & the Qgenda flag should never be cloned (we always want to send to Qgenda).

UAT Enhancement : Clone the Acknowledge Flag field while Cloning the contract

None
 
26/Jan/26 2:43 PM Unified Platform Phase 1 Release Done Provider Contracting Accepted   High Enhancement, SCPHealthCloud, SCPUATTicket   Ashlee Johnson-Savoy Closed ClinicianClientExperience PR1050558-4944
No permission
 Robert Christensen 0 Robert Christensen 2 Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Bug SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project 26/Jan/26 2:43 PM   5 09/Jan/26 5:40 PM SCP Sprint 12.1 (UAT), SCP Sprint 12.2 (Deploy) 11/Jan/26 11:32 PM Logic updated as part of enhancement story 4942
Design: Design Flaws
1|i0j0j7: 29/Jan/26 9:01 PM *Tester:* Erin Bolfer

*Role:* Recruiter

*Test Case ID:* N/A

*Description:* 

An issue was identified while executing a one-time cloned contract, where the rate information did not pass through. We later realized this occurred because the “refresh client rates" button was not clicked during the cloning process. As a result, the contract was cancelled; however, we observed that all contract data was deleted. Upon further analysis, Sakti confirmed this behavior is a defect, as cancelling the contract should not delete all contract elements and data. This issue should be logged as a defect in Jira.

*Steps to Reproduce:*

Existing provider EE Physician with a rate change - used data Troy Antil (479813) @
Ochsner Lafayette General Medical Center-Hospitalist (323420)

*Record Link:* 

None Provided

*Expected vs. Actual:*

An issue was identified while executing a one-time cloned contract, where the rate information did not pass through. We later realized this occurred because the “refresh client rates" button was not clicked during the cloning process. As a result, the contract was cancelled; however, we observed that all contract data was deleted. Upon further analysis, Sakti confirmed this behavior is a defect, as cancelling the contract should not delete all contract elements and data. This issue should be logged as a defect in Jira.

UAT Bug: C&CUAT310 Clone a contract - failed to refresh client rates thus resulting in a canceled contract, but the data was deleted.

None

When I am on a Practice Operations contract
Then the following fields are required on the layout:

# NPPA Tier

04/Feb/26 3:08 PM Unified Platform Phase 1 Release Done Provider Portal Done   Medium TADesignComplete Ryan Woods   PR1050558-5653, PR1050558-5010, PR1050558-5011, PR1050558-811, PR1050558-1789, PR1050558-3546 Tyler Anderson Closed ClinicianClientExperience PR1050558-4907
No permission
 Karthika D AC1: Use Record Triggered Flow

AC2: Use a Flow to validate in User Object 		0 Karthika D 8 Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Story SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project New Enhancement story to ensure that when setting up a Clinician Portal User, the email must be unique to log into the Care Connect/HC Portal.

AC1: Create Task when auto provision of HC Portal access fails (updates story [+PR1050558-1789+|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-1789])
Given I am a Recruiter in the Corporate Support or Corporate Support Console app
When I update the ATS Stage field on an ATS Applicant record to ‘Awaiting Intake Form’
And the clinician has not yet been provisioned HC Portal access
And the system was *unable* to *automatically provisioned HC Portal access* (creation failed due to: clinician ‘Email’ is blank OR clinician ‘Email’ is duplicate to an existing, active HC Portal user)
Then the *system shall automatically create* a task for the user who updated the ATS Stage and instruct them to manually create the user ('Enable Customer User').

* Task Record Type = “Default”
* Assigned To = <insert user who moved the ATS Applicant stage to “Awaiting Intake Form”>
* Subject = “Email Validation Failed – Manually Enable Customer User to Continue”
* Comments = “The system was unable to automatically create a portal user due to the ‘Email’ field on the clinician record being blank or the Email address is a duplicate to an existing, active portal user. The clinician cannot proceed through the recruiting process until this task is complete. Please navigate to the Clinician record and select “Enable Customer User” in the upper right to manually enable the Clinician’s to access the Care Connect portal to view Helpful Links and complete any ‘To Do’ or open tasks. “
* Status = Open
* Due Date = <7 days>
* Priority = High
* Name = <insert Clinician record>
* Related To = <insert ATS Applicant record>

AC2: Add ‘Email’ validation to Manual Creation ‘Enable Customer User' (original story [+PR1050558-811+|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-811]+)+
Given I am a *Recruiter*, *Clinician Experience Team Member*, *Data Quality team member* in the Corporate Support or Corporate Support Console app
When I open a clinician record where they have not been provisioned portal access
And I select the “Enable Customer User” button to manually initiate their portal access (see [security matrix|https://schumachergroup.sharepoint.com/:x:/r/teams/UnifiedPlatform-ReGenesis/Shared%20Documents/Program%20Documentation/Program%20Planning/Discovery%20Deliverables%20%26%20Documentation/Salesforce%20team%20Deliverables/Architecture/Security%20Matrix.xlsx?d=w796b8306c5314622a033076ac7c727e6&csf=1&web=1&e=I8Q1fo] for appropriate user license and profile selections)
And in the *'Email’ field, the email address entered is the same/duplicate of an existing, active portal user*
Then the system shall display the following *error message*:

* “This email address is a duplicate to an existing, active portal user. Please provide a unique email address for this clinician. The clinician cannot proceed through the recruiting process until this step is complete.”

And the user must correct in order to successfully create a new portal user. (Once the Clinician user is enabled, on the Clinician record the button = “Login to Experience as User” ) 		04/Feb/26 3:08 PM   7 Screenshot 2026-01-13 at 10.22.39 AM.png 07/Jan/26 10:13 AM SCP Sprint 12.1 (UAT), SCP Sprint 12.2 (Deploy) 07/Jan/26 10:53 AM 1|i0ivyb: 13/Feb/26 5:18 PM Observation bug _System allows duplicate email during Customer User creation — user unable to log in to portal after logout

Description:

While creating a new *Customer User* from a Clinician record, the system allows using an *email address that is already associated with another Salesforce user*.
Although the user can initially log in to the portal after receiving the credentials, upon logging out and attempting to log in again, the system throws an error and prevents login.

This indicates a *duplicate email handling issue* during Customer User creation.

h3. *Steps to Reproduce:*

# Log in as any *Corporate Support User* in the *Core HC org*.
# Navigate to a *Clinician record*.
# Under the *Highlights panel*, click on *Enable Customer User*.
# Enter an *email address* that is already used by another user in Salesforce.
# Complete the user creation and password recovery process.
# Open the received email from SCP (contains username and temporary password).
# Log in to the portal successfully using:
👉 [https://corehc--qa.sandbox.my.site.com/s|https://corehc--qa.sandbox.my.site.com/s]
# Log out from the portal.
# Attempt to log in again using the same username and password.

h3. *Actual Result:*

* User is *created successfully* even though the email address already exists in the system.
* User is able to *log in initially* to the portal.
* Upon logout, user is *unable to log in again* — system throws a login error.

h3. *Expected Result:*

* The system should *prevent duplicate email addresses* during Customer User creation.
* If a user already exists with the same email, the system should *display an error message* like:
“A user already exists with this email address. Please use a unique email.”
* Portal login should be consistent and functional for newly created users.

Test data

[srishty_sharma23@scphealth.com|mailto:srishty_sharma23@scphealth.com]

!image-20260107-151241.png|width=670,alt="image-20260107-151241.png"!

UAT Enhancement: Prevent Duplicate Email addresses for Active Clinician Portal Users

# NPPA Tier
# Contract Start Date
# Contract End Date 		04/Feb/26 3:10 PM   4 image-20260204-200952.png 07/Jan/26 9:44 AM SCP Sprint 12.1 (UAT), SCP Sprint 12.2 (Deploy) 08/Jan/26 8:24 AM 1|i0ivv7: 11/Mar/26 1:06 PM As a Corporate Support user with access to Practice Operations Contracts, when a Practice Operations contracts is in Status = Executed, then ‘NPPA Tier’, ‘Contract Start Date’ & ‘Contract End Date’ fields should be read only, so that we prevent users from making changes that can disrupt downstream processes and ensure that the user following the process to proper guided maintenance process (Request Contract Change) for updates.

 Kristin Koide Include Status = Superseded in the PO Contract Query that pulls the Rates 0 Kristin Koide 3 Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Story SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project AC1: update PO Contract filter to include Status = Superseded
Given I am a Recruiter or Contacting Specialist within the Corporate Support Apps
When I am working on a Clinician Contract is Status = Draft
And I click on the HPF/Facility, then in the “*Facility Rates as Reference*” component in the right pane on Summary tab
Then the system shall display rates for the specific Facility Title from a Practice Operation contract in Status = “Executed” *or* “*Superseded*”
And same date rules apply for selecting which Practice Operation contract to pull from (Clinician Contract Start Date falls on/within the Practice Operations (PO) contract Contract Start Date).

Example: of current date = {{2026-01-06}}
PO Contract 1 Start Date {{2024-01-01}} - End Date {{2024-12-31}}, Status = Superseded
PO Contract 2 Start Date {{2025-01-01}} - End Date {{2025-12-31}}, Status = Superseded
PO Contract 3 Start Date {{2026-01-01}} - End Date BLANK , Status = Executed

New Clinician Contract Start Date = {{2025-12-01}}, so user will configure rates based on PO Contract 2 (Start Date {{2025-01-01}} - End Date {{2025-12-31}}, Status = Superseded) 		26/Jan/26 3:40 PM   6 image-20260107-105002.png 06/Jan/26 6:09 PM SCP Sprint 12.1 (UAT), SCP Sprint 12.2 (Deploy) 07/Jan/26 5:50 AM 1|i0iv77: 29/Jan/26 9:02 PM C&CUAT285 "No available rates" when trying to configure rates on backdated contract request

*Tester:* Maci Broussard

*Role:* Recruiter

*Test Case ID:* N/A

*Description:* 

Found defect when trying to set up data for agentforce testing. Confirmed this is a defect with Lisa Kleyla. Appears system is telling me there are "no available rates" when Practice Ops contract record has an end date, but Clinician Contract record has an effective date within the PO start and end dates.

*Steps to Reproduce:*

1. Navigate to Clinician person account, where clinician has HPF for Abbeville General Hospital EM.
2. Create a New Contract Request for 12/1/25.
3. Summary - Configure Rates -> Shift
4. Get message "No available rates"

Note: rates for Facility Title are available on PO record, record is just end dated. But we backdate requests often.

*Record Link:* 

[https://corehc--uat2.sandbox.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Contract/800bf00000NF4EDAA1/view?ws=%2Flightning%2Fr%2FAccount%2F001bf00000S47vMAAR%2Fview|https://corehc--uat2.sandbox.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Contract/800bf00000NF4EDAA1/view?ws=%2Flightning%2Fr%2FAccount%2F001bf00000S47vMAAR%2Fview]

*Expected vs. Actual*

Would expect system to display rates from PO (Clinician Contract Start Date falls within start and end date of Practice Ops record)

UAT Enhancement: Clinician Contract - Include PO Contract Status = Superseded - ‘Facility Rates as Reference’ component (4 of 4)

None

PO Contract 2 Start Date {{2025-01-01}} - End Date {{2025-12-31}}, Status = Superseded

Appears system is telling me there are "no available rates" when Practice Ops contract record has an end date, but Clinician Contract record has an effective date within the PO start and end dates.

1. Navigate to Clinician person account, where clinician has HPF for Abbeville General Hospital EM.
2. Create a New Contract Request for 12/1/25.

[https://corehc--uat2.sandbox.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Contract/800bf00000NF4EDAA1/view?ws=%2Flightning%2Fr%2FAccount%2F001bf00000S47vMAAR%2Fview|https://corehc--uat2.sandbox.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Contract/800bf00000NF4EDAA1/view?ws=%2Flightning%2Fr%2FAccount%2F001bf00000S47vMAAR%2Fview]

 Kristin Koide Update the Case flow with the existing platform event (Create or Update a Record Triggers Flow Case for *Update)*

* Condition: Record Type = Clinician Offboarding and Type = SCP is updated so that Status = Closed and Closed Reason = Complete

# Case > Contract > Employee Type = “Employee” Then
## Create a Platform Event record Clinician_Offboarding_Request__e
### Type = “Employee”
### Employee ID = Case.Contact.Employee_Id__c
### Termination Date = Case.Termination_Request_Date__c
### Last Day of Work = Case.Last_Day__c
### Primary Reason = Case.Reason_for_Termination__c
### Reason Description = Case.Description
### Pay Through Date = Case.Last_Day__c
### Rehireable__c = Case.Contact.Rehireable__c
### {color:#ff991f}Global Provider Id = Case.Contact.Global_Provider_ID__c{color}

Add the Global Provider Id Logic 		0 Kristin Koide 3 Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Story SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project AC1:
Given I am an Integration User in the HC org
When we have a case record meeting the following criteria:

* Case Record Type = ‘Clinician Offboarding’
* Type = ‘SCP’
* Employment Type = ‘Employee’ (EE)

AND the Case meeting the above criteria is in Status = Closed and Closed Reason = Complete

Then create a Platform Event, Name = Clinician Offboarding Request, Type = “Employee”:
And add “Global Provider ID” (Global_Provider_Id__c) 		26/Jan/26 2:14 PM   10 image (40).png 17/Dec/25 8:59 AM SCP Sprint 12.1 (UAT), SCP Sprint 12.2 (Deploy) 17/Dec/25 9:41 AM 1|i0ihbf: 29/Jan/26 9:02 PM As an Integration User, I need to know when an EE clinician is leaving SCP, so that I can remove that clinician from Workday.

 Jasmine Moradi PR1050558-4755, PR1050558-4756 Update a record triggered flow for Contract when Contract is Record is created (Fast Field Update)

Check if the Contract has any Executed Contract with Different Type

Query: Select Count(Id) from Contract where Contract.AccountId = NewContract.AccountId AND Contract.RecordType = Clinician

AND Contract.EmploymentType != (NewContract.EmploymentType) AND Contract.Status = Executed {color:#ff991f}*AND StartDate < NewContract.StartDate*{color}

When Count > 0 then Update the newContract.Hire_Date__c = newContract.StartDate 		0 Jasmine Moradi 3 Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Story SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project Given I am a HR team member in the Corporate Support apps
When I navigate to a clinician record where:

* They have a Contract in Executed status with a previously Executed Contract of a different employment type (ANY employment type change qualifies)

Then their hire date is Contract Start Date

And integration sends information to Workday on:

* If First Clinical Shift was blank: when First Clinical Shift is populated (normal process)
* If First Clinical Shift was populated: when the new Contract is Executed (new integration)

1|i0ibtn: 03/Feb/26 11:09 AM *Tester:* Geetha Bhavani Vanam

*Role:* IT

*Test Case ID:* NA

*Description:*

SF HC & MS_ Termination of IC event_ Event should be delayed from SF HC

>>Delay of termination should start from SF HC for termination of IC >> as per Workday

Ideally SF HC should not send event until configured time is met

*Steps to reproduce:*

"Pre Condition :
Facility or Client account is created with all the valid data Associated

Test steps :
1)Clinician is created with valid details(Unique Global provider ID,NPI,Address,email,phone)

2)HPF is created when Clinician is moved to offer with status as Prospect

3)New Contract is created with Facility type EE and Start Admin work flag is set to true

4)Contract is executed and Hire providers event is triggered to workday

After above steps is performed Employee should be created in workday successfully and validate the record at workday"

*Expected vs Actual Result:*

Expected: As per Workday, Delay of termination should start from SF HC for termination of IC >> as per Workday, No event should be triggered from SF HC side

Actual: we are receiving event and its failing at MS with error No supplier found at workday

E2E Bug: C&CUAT236 SF HC & MS_ Termination of IC event_ Event should be delayed from SF HC

30/Jan/26 3:11 PM   Done Client 360 Done   Critical ClientMaintenance, ClinicianImpact, PracticeOperations, SCPUATEnhancement, TADesignComplete Lisa Kleyla   PR1050558-4635, PR1050558-4794, PR1050558-4561, PR1050558-4791, PR1050558-3024, PR1050558-4942 Maci Broussard Closed ClinicianClientExperience PR1050558-4630
No permission
 Kristin Koide {color:#bf2600}Note: Facility Title is one of the KEY but we are processing One Facility Title at a time so that key is not included in the solution and handled at step step 1.a.ii{color}

When the user clicks the “*Refresh Client Rates*”

Apex Code

# For Each ProviderNetworkContract Record in the Clinician Contract
## Get the latest +relevant+ Practice Operation Contract
### ProviderNetworkContract.Healthcare_Practitioner_Facility__r.AccountId = [Contract.AccountId|http://Contract.Id] and [*Contract.*|http://Contract.Id]*StartDate <= ProviderNetworkContract.Contract.StartDate* AND [Contract.|http://Contract.Id]RecordType= Practice Operation and [Contract.|http://Contract.Id]Status = “Executed” Order By [Contract.|http://Contract.Id]StartDate DESC LIMIT 1
### Get all the *Practice Operation* PreventiveCareAgreement record where ContractPaymentAgreement.Type__c = Stipend OR Shift OR Variable OR Target OR Leadership Incentives and Facility_Title__c = ProviderNetworkContract.Healthcare_Practitioner_Facility__r.Facility_Title__c. Loop through each records
#### For ContractPaymentAgreement.Type__c = “Shift”
##### use When Is_Deleted__c = false use [PreventiveCareAgreement.Shift__r.Shift_Full_Name__c-PreventiveCareAgreement.Shift__r.Type__c{color:#ff991f}-PreventiveCareAgreement.Unit_of_Measure__c-PreventiveCareAgreement.Time_Type__c{color}], [PreventiveCareAgreement.Rate__c], [[PreventiveCareAgreement.Id|http://PreventiveCareAgreement.Id]] (use a List<String, Map<Id, Number>>) *[Shift Active Records]*
##### use When Is_Deleted__c = true then use [PreventiveCareAgreement.Shift__r.Shift_Full_Name__c-PreventiveCareAgreement.Shift__r.Type__c{color:#ff991f}-PreventiveCareAgreement.Unit_of_Measure__c-PreventiveCareAgreement.Time_Type__c{color}], [PreventiveCareAgreement.Rate__c], [[PreventiveCareAgreement.Id|http://PreventiveCareAgreement.Id]] (use a List<String, Map<Id, Number>>) *[Shift Deleted Records]*
#### For ContractPaymentAgreement.Type__c != “Shift”
##### use When Is_Deleted__c = false use [PreventiveCareAgreement.Type__c{color:#ff991f}-PreventiveCareAgreement.Unit_of_Measure__c{color}], [PreventiveCareAgreement.Rate__c], [[PreventiveCareAgreement.Id|http://PreventiveCareAgreement.Id]] (use a List<-Id-{color:#ff991f}String{color}, Map<Id, Number>>) *[Other Type Active Records]*
##### use When Is_Deleted__c = true use [PreventiveCareAgreement.Type__c{color:#ff991f}-PreventiveCareAgreement.Unit_of_Measure__c{color}], [PreventiveCareAgreement.Rate__c], [[PreventiveCareAgreement.Id|http://PreventiveCareAgreement.Id]] (use a List<-Id-{color:#ff991f}String{color}, Map<Id, Number>>) *[Other Type Deleted Records]*
## Get all the *Clinician Contract* PreventiveCareAgreement record where -Cloned_From__c != null AND- ContractPaymentAgreement.Type__c = Stipend OR Shift OR Variable OR Target OR Leadership Incentives and Loop through each records
### Process Shift Records
#### For *[Clinician]* ContractPaymentAgreement.Type__c = “Shift” use PreventiveCareAgreement.Shift__r.Shift_Full_Name__c-PreventiveCareAgreement.Shift__r.Type__c{color:#ff991f}-PreventiveCareAgreement.Unit_of_Measure__c-PreventiveCareAgreement.Time_Type__c{color}, PreventiveCareAgreement.Rate__c compare the Rates from Step 1.a.ii.1 and when the [Practice Operation].PreventiveCareAgreement.Rate__c > *[Clinician]*PreventiveCareAgreement.Rate__c then update the *[Clinician]*PreventiveCareAgreement.Rate__c *(use [Shift Active Records] Map)*
#### If there are any shift that is not available in the Practice Operation Contract then delete the Shift from Clinician Contract *(Use [Shift Deleted Records])*
#### Update the *[Clinician].CPA.*Facility_Rate__c to the [*Practice Operation.CAP*].Id *with new Id*
#### {color:#ff991f}Update the {color}{color:#ff991f}*[Clinician].PCA.*{color}{color:#ff991f}Facility_Rate__c to the [{color}{color:#ff991f}*Practice Operation.PCA*{color}{color:#ff991f}].Id {color}{color:#ff991f}*with new Id*{color}
### Process Other Records
#### For *[Clinician]* ContractPaymentAgreement.Type__c != “Shift” use PreventiveCareAgreement.Type__c{color:#ff991f}-PreventiveCareAgreement.Unit_of_Measure__c{color}, PreventiveCareAgreement.Rate__c compare the Rates from Step 1.a.ii.2 and when the *[Practice Operation]*.PreventiveCareAgreement.Rate__c > *[Clinician]*PreventiveCareAgreement.Rate__c then update the *[Clinician]*PreventiveCareAgreement.Rate__c *(Use [Other Type Active Records])*
#### If there are any PreventiveCareAgreement.Type__c{color:#ff991f}-PreventiveCareAgreement.Unit_of_Measure__c{color} that is not available in the Practice Operation Contract then delete from Clinician Contract *(Use [Other Type Deleted Records])*
#### Update the *[Clinician].*Facility_Rate__c to the [*Practice Operation .CPA*].Id *when its same*
#### {color:#ff991f}Update the {color}{color:#ff991f}*[Clinician].PCA.*{color}{color:#ff991f}Facility_Rate__c to the [{color}{color:#ff991f}*Practice Operation.PCA*{color}{color:#ff991f}].Id {color}{color:#ff991f}*with new Id*{color}
## Once complete update today to Contract.Last_Synched_Date__c

Button should be visibly only for Draft Clinician Contract



*Copy Time Type:* From Practice Operation Contract to Clinician Contract when when PCA shifts are copied over to Clinician PCA copy *PreventiveCareAgreement*.Time_Type__c 		0 Kristin Koide 8 Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Story SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project This story enhances original story [https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-3024|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-3024|smart-link]. All existing requirements apply, we are just updating the matching logic for how we identify a match between the Clinician Contract & Practice Operations contract.

AC1: *Clinician Contract*
Given I am an Contracting specialist/Recruiter within the Corporate Support Apps
When I am on a Clinician contract in Status = Draft
And on the Summary tab, I click “*Refresh Client Rates*”
Then for each Clinician Rate, the system shall check for a matching Practice Operations contract record; a match is defined as same value in each of the specified fields per Type of Rate:

* Type of Rate = Shift
** Facility Title
** Shift (Full Shift Name)
** Shift Type (NP/PA, MD/DO, or RN/LPN)
** Unit of Measure (*NEW* matching criteria)
** Time Type (*NEW* matching criteria; used for matching only if value exists.)
* Type of Rate = Variable
** Facility Title
** Type
** Unit of Measure (*NEW* matching criteria)
* Type of Rate = Stipend
** Facility Title
** Type
** Unit of Measure (*NEW* matching criteria)
* Type of Rate = Leadership Incentive
** Facility Title
** Type
** Unit of Measure (*NEW* matching criteria)
* Type of Rate = Target
** Facility Title
** Type
** Unit of Measure (*NEW* matching criteria) 		30/Jan/26 3:11 PM   8 image-20260108-155114.png 11/Dec/25 5:47 PM SCP Sprint 12.1 (UAT), SCP Sprint 12.2 (Deploy) 16/Dec/25 5:02 AM 1|i0ian7: 30/Jan/26 3:11 PM As a Clinician Contracting Specialist, when I select “Refresh Client Rates” on a Draft Clinician Contract, I want the system to match and update/remove rates based on the attached matching criteria, so that I am working with the most updated Client rates.



[^Matching Criteria per Type of Rate_ PR1050558-4561 - Sheet1.pdf]

 15/Dec/25

UAT Enhancement: Update matching logic for "Refresh Client Rates"

None

 Robert Christensen PR1050558-5159, PR1050558-5160 Update the formula fields 0 Robert Christensen 2 Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Story SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project Given I am a Legal team member in the Corporate Support apps
When I navigate to a client contract
And I populate Agreement Effective Date, Agreement Start Date, Contract Term (Months), Renewal Notice Date, Internal Renewal Start Date
Then the following fields automatically populate based on the calculations specified:

* Agreement End Date = separate user story [https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-5102|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-5102|smart-link]
* Renewal Notice Date = IF *Renewal Notice Days* is blank, use *Agreement End Date*, otherwise use *Agreement End Date - Renewal Notice Days*
* Internal Renewal Start Date = IF *Internal Renewal Notification Days* is blank, use *Agreement End Date*, otherwise use *Renewal Notice Date* - *Internal Renewal Notification Days* 		03/Feb/26 2:52 PM   10 Screenshot 2026-01-23 at 12.38.59 AM.png 11/Dec/25 5:26 PM SCP Sprint 12.1 (UAT), SCP Sprint 12.2 (Deploy) 12/Dec/25 4:03 AM 1|i0iamj: 16/Feb/26 4:54 PM As a Legal team member, I need Agreement End Date to be automatically populated, so that data is accurately captured for client contracts

*Tester:* Anna Lewis Elledge
*Role:* Legal - Client Contracting
*Test Case ID:* N/A
*Description:* "When working in the Configurations tab on a Client Contract, under Terms and Renewals the following should occur:

# Enter Agreement Effective Date
# Enter Agreement Start Date
# Enter Contract Term (Months)

Upon completion of entering data, 'Agreement End Date' should automatically populate. Likewise, the Renewal Notice Date and Internal Renewal Start Date should also automatically populate based on the information entered in the 'Renewal Notice Days' and 'Internal Renewal Notification Days' fields.

*Record Link:* [https://corehc--uat2.sandbox.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Contract/800bf00000MIdB7AAL/view?ws=%2Flightning%2Fr%2FAccount%2F001bf00000S3zr3AAB%2Fview|https://corehc--uat2.sandbox.lightning.force.com/lightning/r/Contract/800bf00000MIdB7AAL/view?ws=%2Flightning%2Fr%2FAccount%2F001bf00000S3zr3AAB%2Fview]

 Jasmine Moradi AC1: The solution is similar of how the NPPA Tier is auto populated or user entered when there is a mismatch.

Check if the Contract.Employment_Type__c is Employee Contract.Time_Type__c is Full Time, and clinician Contract.Account.Professional_Title__c title is NP or PA (same as new Contract use case no change here)

When all the NPPA Tier has the same value then defalut Contract.NPPA_Tier__c = Client PO contract tier else force the user to input NPPA Tier (similar solution for new Contract)

!image-20251209-195436.png|width=917,alt="image-20251209-195436.png"!



AC2: When cloning the contract clone the fields below Contract Fields

* NPPA Tier
* EE NP/PA Full Time Weekly Blended
* EE NP/PA Hourly Blended
* EE NP/PA Full Time Overage Rate
* EE NP/PA Full Time Monthly Blended



AC3: Re Calculate NPPA Tier field (already built) when PreventiveCareAgreement.Is_Deleted__c = false or PreventiveCareAgreement.Cloned_From__c = Null or PreventiveCareAgreement.Cloned_From__c != Null (new change) and exclude when PreventiveCareAgreement.ContractPayementAgreement.Provider_Network_Contract__c.Is_Deleted__c = true (deleted use case for facility title change - Contract PNC Gray use case)

* EE NP/PA Full Time Weekly Blended
* EE NP/PA Hourly Blended
* EE NP/PA Full Time Overage Rate
* EE NP/PA Full Time Monthly Blended 		0 Jasmine Moradi 13 Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Story SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project AC1: Given I am a Recruiter in the corporate support apps
When I request a contract for a *full time employee NP/PA* clinician (Contract’s Employment Type = ‘Employee’ AND Contract’s Time Type = ‘Full Time’, and the Clinician’s Professional Title = ‘NP’ or ‘PA’)
And I am either +creating the contract+ OR +adding additional HPFs+
Then I can see the NP/PA Tier value from the client practice operations contract
And I can specify NP/PA Tier for that clinician:

* If Service Accounts from selected HPFs have the same NP/PA Tier
** Default the value of the Clinician Contract record NP/PA Tier field
** User selection is NOT needed since there is only one possible value
* If Service Accounts from selected HPFs do not have the same NP/PA Tier
** Recruiter must select one NP/PA Tier from what values (unique values) are available at those facilities. (Design is to implement an validation rules with error if the user selects a NPPA tier values that is NOT applicable = “Please select an NPPA Tier value based on your chosen facilities”.
*** Display a message that Upon Submit Contract Request, then an approval will be sent to review NP/PA Tier mismatch
** And an approval record is created for Account Team Regional Director of NPPA Services to review NP/PA Tier mismatch (NOTE: upon clicking ‘Submit Contract Request’ button, then send approval)

AC2: Given I am a Recruiter in the corporate support apps
When I clone an Executed Clinician Contract (selecting “Start from Previously Active Contract” in Contract Request OS)
Then the following fields are cloned:

* NPPA Tier
* EE NP/PA Full Time Weekly Blended
* EE NP/PA Hourly Blended
* EE NP/PA Full Time Overage Rate
* EE NP/PA Full Time Monthly Blended

AC3: And if I add new Facilities to the Clinician Contract

Then the newly added and cloned Facilities are included in the EE NP/PA field re-calculations exclude deleted PNC from the Calculation 		04/Feb/26 3:22 PM   5 image-20251209-195436.png 09/Dec/25 9:18 AM SCP Sprint 12.1 (UAT), SCP Sprint 12.2 (Deploy) 15/Dec/25 3:56 AM 1|i0i4oz: 04/Feb/26 3:22 PM As a Clinician Contract Specialist, I need to view NP/PA tiers and where clinicians are expected to practice, so that I can generate contracts accurately

 Jasmine Moradi PR1050558-4429 0 Jasmine Moradi 5 Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Story SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project Current Design:

# Customer Community Login - Prospect Clinician
# Customer Community Plus - Employee Clinician
# Customer Community Plus Login - Reserved for Internal Employees

Needs to be updated since #1 is no longer available. For Prospect Clinicians we need to use Customer Community Plus Login license going forward.

Note changes to:

* UserAccessPolicy
* SharingSet
* Community Administration
* AtAGlance IPs- (If we are changing profile name too)

 Kristin Koide PR1050558-4238, PR1050558-4239 AC1: Record Triggered Flow in Object Task, update the Client Change Management records

AC2: Update Sharing Setting in Field

AC3: Enable Tracking History 		0 Kristin Koide 3 Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Story SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project AC1: Auto update the Client Change Management record Status
Given I am a Clinician Experience Specialist within the Corporate Support apps
When a Client Change Management record is created with Process Type = Manual and Status = New (original story for task creation [+PR1050558-1682+|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-1682]+.+ Related story for additional CCM record creation [PR1050558-3574|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-3574])
And I receive a task subject = “Create New Clinician Contract for [Clinician’s Name]”
And I update the Task Status
Then

* If Task Status = ‘Completed’, then the system shall update the related Client Change Management record Status = ‘Completed’
* If Task Status = ‘Canceled’, then the system shall update the related Client Change Management record Status = ‘Canceled’


AC2: Update Sharing Setting
Given I am a System Administrator
When I am in Setup
And Object = Client Change Management
Then the Sharing Setting for ‘Contract’ field is set to:

* Read Only: Allows users with at least Read access to the Master record to create, edit, or delete related Detail records.



AC3: Enable History Tracking for ‘Status’
Given I am a System Administrator
When I am in Setup
And Object = Client Change Management
Then History Tracking is enabled for Field = 'Status' 		02/Feb/26 3:51 PM   3 17/Nov/25 1:21 PM SCP Sprint 12.1 (UAT), SCP Sprint 12.2 (Deploy), SCP Sprint 12 18/Nov/25 10:08 AM 1|i0h83v: 10/Feb/26 12:30 PM As a Clinician Experience Specialist, when I close my task “Create New Clinician Contract for [Clinician’s Name]', then I want the system to update the status of the related Client Change Management record, so that when users report on the Client Change Management records they know if it was completed or canceled.

Client Change Management - Update Client Change Management Status based on Task Status

None

 Kristin Koide AC 1
In the existing Applicant_After_Insert_After_Update_Flow,
//Check if inactive user exists for that Applicant Account.
Get user record where AccountId = Applicant Id ({!$Record.bpats__ATS_Applicant__r.Account.}) and User.IsActive = false.
if record exists, update the user record by setting Active = true and Email = {!$Record.bpats__ATS_Applicant__r.Account.Email}
Add Send email action with Reset password template specified in AC

AC 4
On the existing flow, that sends out platform event, update the condition to include HPF status _Candidate in OR condition_


!image-20251113-161209.png|width=817,alt="image-20251113-161209.png"!




 0 Kristin Koide 5 Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Story SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project Story split from 3573, this story is to track *testing only* of AC2 & AC3. *Dependent on Okta Team to complete work in QA.*

– For AC 2 &3 can be tested in an environment where Okta is set up. No work in SF, just needs to be tested in QA. Need to coordinate with the Okta team - Sravan Kumar Naladala –

AC2: Remove SSO Permission
Given I am a Clinician that left SCP
When Okta deactivated the Experience Cloud User
Then Okta shall also ensure that the SSO Permission set has been removed

AC3: Add SSO Permission
Given I am a Clinician (Active) that has an executed contract and contract’s related HPF(s) Provider System Status = ‘Active’ {color:#bf2600}*or ‘Candidate’*{color} (part of story [+PR1050558-3614+|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-3614]+,+ update in {color:#bf2600}red{color} based on AC4)
When Okta has update the ‘Federation ID’ & ‘Email’ fields on the Experience Cloud User
Then Okta shall ensure that the SSO Permission set has been added
And from Portal Login, the user will input their email
And system directs to the SSO login (story [+PR1050558-3546+|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-3546])



----

NOTE: SF work AC 1 & AC 4 are part of story [https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-3573|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-3573|smart-link]

AC1: Reactivate Experience Cloud User in Salesforce

AC4: Given I am an Integration User in the HC org (updates story [+PR1050558-3614+|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-3614]+,+ updates in {color:#bf2600}red{color})

Assumptions:

# Upon initial User Deactivation:
## Okta will deactivate the Okta User
## Okta will deactivate the Experience Cloud User & remove the access.
## The SCP Team has either automatically or manually cleared the Experience Cloud User’s ‘Federation ID’ field for Phase 1.
# Upon reapply/rehire:
## The Clinician has one person account linked to one user record
# Upon Clinician contract Executed: (see story [+PR1050558-3614+|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-3614])
## The SCP/Okta Team has updated the Create API to reactivate the Okta User via the Global Provider ID.
### If there is a Global Provider ID, then Okta will send the appropriate activation email.
### If there is NO Global Provider ID, then Okta will create the new user and appropriate email is sent
## Okta activates Okta User
## Okta updates the Experience Cloud User’s ‘Federation ID’ & ‘Email’

----

REFINEMENT NOTES

{color:#bf2600}Question:{color}

# {color:#bf2600}Jose - As part of AC3 which is related to story {color}[https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-3614|https://salesforce.atlassian.net/browse/PR1050558-3614|smart-link]{color:#bf2600}, as part of our new criteria trigger the platform event, in addition to the Clinician’s Contract Status = Executed, we are also checking if contracts {color}{color:#bf2600}*related HPF(s) ‘Provider System Status’ field to equal ‘Active’. Are we sure that we want to include this new criteria?*{color}{color:#bf2600} {color}{color:#0747a6}*11/11, we will leave this criteria on story 3614. Jose will check with business if this criteria needs to be adjusted and if so will adjust ‘Provider System Status’ formula field to make the status = “Active” at the right time.*{color}{color:#0747a6} {color}

{color:#bf2600}There is current automation the HPF ‘Status’ is set to ‘Active’, when the ‘First Clinical Shift’ is populated. However, it is towards the end of the onboarding process. It seems a bit late in the process to wait for the 'Provider System Status’ field to equal ‘Active’ to provision Okta.{color}

Screenshot showing where Contract Status = Executed and where the HPF *Status* = Active.

!image-20251107-192120.png|width=1017,height=183,alt="image-20251107-192120.png"!

Also the E2E says Okta SSO is provisioned when Contract Status = Executed (screenshot below is a close up shot of the first green box).

!image-20251107-192719.png|width=313,height=183,alt="image-20251107-192719.png"! 		02/Feb/26 10:35 AM   5 image-20251107-192120.png 17/Nov/25 9:56 AM SCP Sprint 12.1 (UAT), SCP Sprint 12.2 (Deploy), SCP Sprint 12 17/Nov/25 9:56 AM 1|i0h4ob: 02/Feb/26 10:35 AM As a System Administrator, when a Clinician leaves SCP (deactivated) and decides to reapply/rehire, I want the system to reactivate the Clinician’s previous Experience Cloud User record instead of creating a new user record, so that we keep our system clean.

As a System Administrator, I want to ensure that when a clinician’s user has the appropriate permission, the system can direct them to the appropriate sign-in method. 		14/Nov/25

Tech - SSO Flow - TESTING ONLY - Okta to Add/Remove SSO Permission

None

04/Feb/26 3:30 PM Unified Platform Phase 1 Release Done Scheduling and Pay Done   Low SFQACompleted, TADesignComplete Lisa Kleyla   PR1050558-3779 Maci Broussard Closed ClinicianClientExperience PR1050558-4088

 Samudhraa Samudhraa PR1050558-4248, PR1050558-4249 0 Samudhraa Samudhraa 5 Restricted to agents Restricted to agents 0 Story SCP Health - All In Proservices Digital Transformation-Project Ensure when PCAs are deleted ( because shifts on PO Contract are deleted or PO PCA is deleted or any other scenario), the rates are recalculated and reflect only the existing PCA records that are applicable.

Given I am a Payroll team member in the Corporate Support apps
When EE NP/PA rates are specified for a clinician (using per hour unit of measure)
Then the following calculations are done automatically:

* EE NP/PA Full Time Weekly Blended
** Hourly Shift Rates averaged across per Facility * 40
* EE NP/PA Hourly Blended
** Hourly Shift Rates averaged across per Facility HPFs
* EE NP/PA Full Time Overage Rate
** Hourly Shift Rates averaged across per Facility HPFs + $2
* EE NP/PA Full Time Monthly Blended
** EE NP/PA Hourly Blended * NP/PA Tier

*see the attached document*
*The NPPA rates only apply to Shift records, where Shift are of Unit of measure = per hour*

additional notes:
_FT EE NP/PA clinicians have one monthly salary blended in contract_
_PT EE NP/PA clinicians have one hourly blended rate in contract_
_for FT and PT - QGenda stores blended hourly rate_
_for FT - Workday stores annual salary_
_for PT - Workday stores hourly blended rate_
if clinician has Night Shift Bonus, sent to QGenda and applies only for some shifts

[^Calculations.xlsx]
